title: Rei has Aspergers High Functioning Autism NLD
display_title: Rei has Aspergers High Functioning Autism NLD
wikitext:
[]: Rei has Aspergers / High Functioning Autism / NLD             Quote Post by Ricky O » Mon Jul 18, 2011 6:23 pm Just THROWING THIS OUT THERE. Bland affect. Unable to relate to others. Difficulty with personal space. Difficulties understanding non-verbal cues. Lack of perspective on "the big picture." Seemingly emotionless, emotions at the wrong times. Sweet and caring deep down (this one's mine). Okay, so, DISCUSS.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Mon Jul 18, 2011 6:33 pm A lot of that actually sounds like the manga Kaworu to me. I've never really considered a legitimate reason for Rei deattachment, other than her being a clone/lack of normality. She was raised for all the wrong reasons..but when you lay it out like that it certainly does add up.

[]:             Quote Post by Ricky O » Mon Jul 18, 2011 6:42 pm She's most definitely not normal, and probably could have endured any number of developmental disabilities. I think it makes sense. I think EVA would be one of the only animes to actually acknowledge Autism and its forms in any way.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Mon Jul 18, 2011 7:17 pm She doesn't have Asperger's. Her autism is far worse/better then that.

[]:             Quote Post by Yue » Mon Jul 18, 2011 7:26 pm I doubt Rei's psychological state could be defined in terms that mostly refer to Lilin illnesses. She's screwed up because Gendo is the closest she had to a father, she only has half a soul, and oh right that soul belongs to a seed of life.

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon Jul 18, 2011 7:55 pm I say the way she acts is really because of the living environment she was raised in(?), as well as whatever Gendo was doing to her according to what's her in bathroom cabinet

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Jul 18, 2011 8:05 pm Hang on to your hats folks, here we go again.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Mon Jul 18, 2011 8:55 pm Oh boy!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Jul 18, 2011 8:59 pm We actually did have a discussion about this here .

[]: Re: Rei has Aspergers / High Functioning Autism / NLD             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Tue Jul 19, 2011 2:03 am Ricky O wrote: Just THROWING THIS OUT THERE. Bland affect. Unable to relate to others. Difficulty with personal space. Difficulties understanding non-verbal cues. Lack of perspective on "the big picture." Seemingly emotionless, emotions at the wrong times.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:12 am Hm, i don't think that Yui had anything like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 19, 2011 3:17 am Repopotamus from the other aspie thread (with rewording for certain clarifications): Monk Ed wrote: Rei strikes me as being too "cool" to be one. Fitting a checklist of characteristics is one thing, but there is a qualitative aspect as well. There's a certain way how to be socially awkward that indicates Aspergers. Maybe if Rei told more jokes that only she understands, spoke in non-sequiturs, or had a tendency to babble on about shit she cares about but nobody else in the room does, it'd be a more convincing case. I think her diagnosis bends closer towards schizoid than Asperger's, if we're hunting for names to apply.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaPilot10 » Tue Jul 19, 2011 10:05 am Monk Ed wrote: ....I'm not sure if AS is purely genetic in origin -- if so, it would suggest that Rei would be one only if Yui was too.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Tue Jul 19, 2011 10:32 am EvaPilot10 wrote: AS does have genetic factors but it's not purely genetic in origin. There are several people who don't have AS but have given birth to children who were diagnosed with AS.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaPilot10 » Tue Jul 19, 2011 10:37 am Someone wrote: But cloning = exactly the same DNA.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 19, 2011 11:04 am EvaPilot10 wrote: EDIT: Since when did Rei have exactly the same DNA as Yui? I thought part of her DNA came from Lilith?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Tue Jul 19, 2011 1:37 pm Rei only appears autistic because her thought process and mannerisms are, more or less, those of an alien being. Trying to judge her by the standards of human social interaction is like trying to judge a dolphin by the same standards.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jul 19, 2011 4:26 pm Bagheera wrote: It does.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 19, 2011 4:41 pm Monk Ed wrote: Debate remains on that.

[]:             Quote Post by Cross of Iron » Tue Jul 19, 2011 7:48 pm Bagheera wrote: It does. Also, she doesn't have Asperger's...per our resident shrink (Brikhaus) she has schizoid personality disorder if anything, and even that's sketchy given her circumstances and the fact that she's an anime character (and thus necessarily lacks the depth of a real person, no matter how complex she may be by fictional standards). Don't forget that she's extremely isolated socially and all of four years old. Comparing her to a normal human is...not appropriate.

Difficulty with personal space. Difficulties understanding non-verbal cues. Lack of perspective on "the big picture." Seemingly emotionless, emotions at the wrong times. Sweet and caring deep down (this one's mine). Okay, so, DISCUSS.
